title: General audience abstract
generation_instructions: Write a non-technical summary of the proposed research project that is accessible to a general audience. Clearly articulate the problem being addressed, the proposed solution, and its potential impact on melanoma prevention, detection, diagnosis, or treatment. Avoid technical jargon, and clearly articulate the practical implications and potential benefits of the proposed research.
search_queries: melanoma prevention detection and treatment public impact

title: Technical abstract
generation_instructions: Provide a concise technical summary of the proposed research project, including the specific aims, research methods, and expected outcomes. Clearly state the hypothesis, the experimental design, and the analytical techniques that will be used. Make sure this is well aligned with the project description. Be specific, use scientific language, and focus on the technical aspects of the project.

title: Project description
generation_instructions: Provide a comprehensive description of the proposed research project. This section should integrate the background, specific aims, preliminary data, experimental design and methods, statistical plan, figures, and rationale. Clearly articulate how the proposed research addresses a gap in translational science, has the potential to produce high-impact, near-term advancements in melanoma, and addresses a critical need. Be concise, focus on the research specifics, and demonstrate a clear understanding of the scientific landscape and the clinical challenges. This section must integrate the following sub-sections, figures can be embedded.

title: Background and specific aims
generation_instructions: Provide a detailed background of the research, including an overview of the current state of the field, relevant gaps in knowledge, and the rationale for your study. Clearly define the specific aims of the project. Explain how the specific aims of the project will address gaps in translational science and improve prevention, detection, diagnosis, and treatment of melanoma. Provide the rationale behind each aim and explicitly state its impact and translational potential.

title: Preliminary data
generation_instructions: Present any preliminary data that support the feasibility and rationale of the proposed research. The data should be clear and relevant to the proposed research aims. Explain how the preliminary data was obtained, and its significance. Emphasize how this data supports the viability of your experimental approach and the likelihood of achieving your specific aims.

title: Experimental design and methods
generation_instructions: Provide a clear and detailed description of the experimental design and methods that will be used to conduct the proposed research. Include the step-by-step protocol, control experiments, and any relevant techniques. Ensure that your description includes all the necessary details to enable reproducibility and to evaluate the rigor of the study design.

title: Statistical plan
generation_instructions: Describe the statistical methods that will be used to analyze the data. Detail the statistical tests, sample size justification, and any measures that are in place to ensure data validity. Clearly justify the choices in statistical methods and show that the plan is aligned with the research design and the research aims.

title: Figures
generation_instructions: Include relevant figures, diagrams, or charts to visualize the proposed research and results. Figures should be clear, well-labeled, and directly pertinent to the research. Embed the figures within the project description as appropriate, with accompanying legends that explain what each figure represents, as well as an interpretation of the presented data.

title: Rationale/fit with key criteria
generation_instructions: Provide a clear explanation of how the proposed research fits with the key criteria outlined in the call for proposals. Specifically, discuss the projects relevance to the funder, including alignment with priorities related to melanoma prevention, detection, diagnosis, and/or treatment. Explain how the project has the potential for high impact and near-term advancements.

title: Potential for clinical impact
generation_instructions: Discuss the potential for the proposed research to have a clinical impact on melanoma prevention, detection, diagnosis, or treatment. Explain how the findings can be translated into clinical practice, leading to improvements in patient outcomes. Highlight how the research is designed to address a specific gap in clinical care or patient management. Be sure to articulate how the project will be translated into tangible benefits for high-risk individuals and/or patients with melanoma.
